title: `podman-restart.service` stop containers when systemd service is running
body:
### Issue Description

When exiting, `podman-restart.service` stops all containers that are flagged to start after boot. This is fine for the `always` restart policy, but for `unless-stopped`, it behaves just like a user running `podman stop` <container>.

```
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman  $LOGGING stop  --all --filter should-start-on-boot=true
```
Note that stopping the service will automatically stop all running containers that match `--filter should-start-on-boot=true` on your machine.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
podman run -d --restart=unless-stopped --name my_container fedora sleep infinity
# Check container if it is running:
podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND         CREATED        STATUS         PORTS       NAMES
58ae1d244603  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  sleep infinity  2 minutes ago  Up 21 seconds              my_container

systemctl --user enable podman-restart.service
systemctl --user start podman-restart.service
# Check if service is running: 
systemctl --user status podman-restart.service

reboot

# Check the container if it is running after reboot:
podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND         CREATED        STATUS         PORTS       NAMES
```


### Describe the results you received

A container with `--restart=unless-stopped` does not start after a machine reboot, if `podman-restart.service` was running before the restart. 

### Describe the results you expected

A container with `--restart=unless-stopped` does start after a machine reboot.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

Additional environment details

### Additional information
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20418
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27619

comments.body: Maybe would could have a hidden cli flag or maybe env var to not set the stopped by user bool? We would only need to support that for local podman in the systemd unit so need to worry about API/podman-remote

That's a good idea. My first thought on how to fix this is to drop this line from `podman-restart.service`:
`ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman  $LOGGING stop  --all --filter should-start-on-boot=true`

Because when I run `systemctl --user stop podman-restart.service`, it stops all containers that match the filter `--filter should-start-on-boot=true`.  I am not sure if this is intentional behavior.

WDYT? @Luap99

I think the original problem was that without that line podman container cleanup just keeps restarting the container as per the restart policy, https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14446

So that either blocks the shutdown and/or means podman is killed ungracefully eventually which may corrupt state. The explicit stop would solve that. But I guess if we stop setting the bool we might be in the same restart loop, so that would not help
